description_long: This object is a vintage-style television with a boxy, rectangular body finished in a deep red color. The front features a convex glass screen bordered by a thick, silver bezel, with two large rotary dials and a speaker grille on the right side. Two thin, metallic antenna rods extend upward from the top, giving it a classic retro appearance. The back panel is brown and perforated for ventilation, with a power cord emerging from the lower section and looping outward. The sides are smooth with small access panels, and the television stands on four short, sturdy legs. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century analog televisions commonly found in homes and urban environments.
description: A retro red television with a convex glass screen, silver bezel, rotary dials, and twin antennas, standing on four legs with a visible power cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, silver bezel, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and both antennas.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the body, the protruding back, and the antennas, with the power cord visible trailing backward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the brown perforated back panel, both antennas, and the full length of the power cord.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the smooth red surface, access panel, and the power cord extending from the back.
attribute_bezel_design: thick silver bezel, metallic finish, rectangular with rounded corners
attribute_control_button_layout: two rotary dials and a speaker grille in a vertical cluster on the front right
